title: Webhook management
excerpt: We have come to realize that we do need more automations than what we get by default from monday, so we have been using webhooks to make.com so we can make more complex automations, but the more webhooks we need/create i…

posts.author: elcloche (Alejandro Graffe)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have come to realize that we do need more automations than what we get by default from monday, so we have been using webhooks to make.com so we can make more complex automations, but the more webhooks we need/create it starts to get more dificult to manage.

We think there should be a place where we can manage all webhooks, where we can view the link, add a name and a short description of what will be triggered on the other end.

As for now if I want to send a webhook there is a very limited view of the webhooks already used with no name or descrition, so we do not know if the webhook we already have there works.
